title: galactites duriaei
intro: Galactites duriaei, also known as Milk Thistle Knapweed, is a flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate milk production in lactating women and its potential as an anti-inflammatory agent. The plant is rich in saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and hepatoprotective effects, making it beneficial for liver and digestive health. Galactites duriaei can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used to support lactation, alleviate digestive issues, and protect against liver damage.
